title: Deaths due to unsafe sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [unsafe sex](#dod:unsafe_sex). Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.
caption:
Deaths due to unsafe sex

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.

The choropleth map displays the estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex in 2023, using a color scale ranging from light beige for zero deaths to dark maroon for 300,000 deaths. The highest values, falling within the 100,000 to 300,000 band, are concentrated in Southern Africa, specifically South Africa, and in South Asia, where India is shaded in the darkest tone. China and parts of Eastern Europe appear in the 30,000 to 100,000 range, represented by a medium-dark orange. Across North America, the United States sits in the 10,000 to 30,000 band, while Canada and Mexico are in the lower 3,000 to 10,000 range. Most of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, falls between the 3,000 and 30,000 bands. In Africa, aside from the southern tip, countries like Nigeria and Ethiopia are visible in the 10,000 to 30,000 range, while much of the rest of the continent is distributed between the 1,000 and 10,000 bands. Australia and Greenland appear in the lightest shade, indicating values near zero or within the 0 to 1,000 range. Several countries in the Middle East and Central Asia also display the lightest shading. The map includes a "No data" category in the legend, though no specific countries are clearly hatched with this pattern in the main view. The visualization highlights a distinct geographic concentration of higher mortality figures in specific regions of Africa and Asia compared to the lower figures observed in Oceania and parts of the Americas.
